philippines.title: Cebu City to give 135,000 learners school supplies before class opening
philippines.description:
LAPU-LAPU CITY, Cebu — Around 135,000 students in Cebu City will receive school supplies and learning materials from the city government ahead of the opening of classes for school year 2026-2027. The distribution forms part of the city’s education support program funded through the Local School Board as public schools prepare to reopen on June 8. City officials said that each learner from Grades 1 to 10 will receive a backpack containing notebooks, pencils, and erasers. READ: EXPLAINER: How Brigada Eskwela sets public schools for academic year Students from Cambinocot Elementary School were among the first recipients during the launch […]...
Keep on reading: Cebu City to give 135,000 learners school supplies before class opening

philippines.title: Brigada Eskwela 2026: Parents, teachers brace for new school year
philippines.description:
CEBU CITY, Philippines — Before public schools open each year, the Brigada Eskwela program gathers parents, teachers, and community volunteers to clean up classrooms, fix school furniture, and give facilities a fresh coat of paint. But more than that, it’s also a signal for parents and teachers to make the financial and mental preparations for a new school year. This year, the adjustments will include a new trimestral calendar. Cebu City Central Elementary School Principal Anatolia Patosa said the school is preparing an orientation for parents once classes open. This is likely to take place within the first week of […]...
Keep on reading: Brigada Eskwela 2026: Parents, teachers brace for new school year

philippines.title: 'A boycott for Jinggoy': Senate minority pans majority's absence
philippines.description:
The Senate minority bloc said the absence of the majority during Monday’s session was a boycott following the arrest of Sen. Jinggoy Estrada, not a call for Senate independence as Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano claimed....

energy.title: Oil Surges Over 7% as Iran Halts U.S. Talks and Threatens Total Hormuz Closure
energy.description: UPDATED 10:20 EST, MONDAY JUNE 1ST: Oil prices exploded higher Monday, with U.S. crude leaping more than 7% after Iran’s state-affiliated media reported that Tehran is cutting off diplomatic channels with Washington and moving to fully block the strategic Strait of Hormuz. The sudden breakdown in diplomacy shattered fragile market optimism, reversing earlier trading dynamics and sending energy markets into a tailspin. WTI crude surged 7.69% to approach $93 per barrel, while Brent crude jumped 6.62%, trading well above $95 per barrel as investors…
